vulnerability.title: Ensure nss-tools is installed
vulnerability.description: The nss-tools package can be installed with the following command: $ sudo dnf install nss-tools
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: if ! rpm -q --quiet "nss-tools" ; then dnf install -y "nss-tools" fi

triage_decision.reason: The finding requires the installation of a standard utility package (nss-tools) via the package manager. Installing a tool package is a low-risk operation that does not modify existing configurations or disrupt system services.

previous_attempts.scan_output: Single-rule scan failed for package_nss-tools_installed (rule=xccdf_org.ssgproject.content_rule_package_nss-tools_installed). Could not verify.

llm_verdict.message: The `nss-tools` package was confirmed to be missing via `rpm -q nss-tools` and was subsequently installed using `dnf install -y nss-tools`. A final verification check confirms that `nss-tools-3.112.0-4.el9_4.x86_64` is now installed on the system.

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The plan correctly addresses the vulnerability by checking for the existence of the package and installing it via the system package manager if missing.
suggested_improvements: Ensure the system is connected to a valid repository during execution.
